summary: Backend Refactor Phase 2 - REGRESSION TEST PASSED. All 30 tests pass. Availability routes (GET/PUT availability, bulk update) and Hold routes (create/release/extend hold, get venue/lead holds) migrated from server.py to routes/availability.py work correctly. Phase 1 migrations (auth, venues) also verified working. Business logic in services/availability_service.py functioning correctly.

minor.issue: DateHoldRequest model has redundant venue_id field - it's both a path parameter and required in request body
minor.suggestion: Consider removing venue_id from DateHoldRequest body since it's already in the URL path, or use the path value only

action_items: OPTIONAL: Remove venue_id from DateHoldRequest model body since it's already in URL path parameter (API works, but this is redundant)
critical_code_review_comments: routes/availability.py: Clean implementation with proper auth (require_role) and audit logging
services/availability_service.py: Well-structured service layer with auto-release of expired holds
Hold extension logic correctly limits to 2 extensions for non-admins
Time remaining calculation in get_lead_holds correctly handles timezone-aware datetimes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactor Phase 2 regression test complete. Availability and Hold routes successfully migrated from server.py to modular routes/availability.py with business logic in services/availability_service.py. All 11 endpoints tested: (1) Availability: GET availability, PUT availability, POST bulk update (2) Holds: POST hold-date, DELETE hold-date, POST extend, GET venue holds, GET lead holds (3) Phase 1 verified: Auth routes, Venue routes (4) Legacy routes verified: leads, control-room, payments. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_availability_holds_regression.py
